search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng182 insect bites assessment skin reaction reassessment
candidate_sources_rejected: NICE antihistamine and antimicrobial treatment recommendations
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Treatment, medicine, dose, infection conclusion, referral, and urgency content were not mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with a suspected insect bite or sting; tick, travel, allergy, mouth, throat, eye, pregnancy, and immunocompromise contexts need qualified assessment.
setting_applicability: UAE community, primary care, allergy, infectious-disease, and emergency assessment.
UAE_applicability: Assessment principles are transferable, but UAE insect and travel epidemiology, allergy, infection, medicine, emergency, and referral pathways are required.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG182 recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NICE NG182 remains the current official guideline identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact assessment section supports documenting an insect bite or sting context, onset, local inflammatory or allergic symptoms, distribution, infection features and systemic-reaction assessment without inferring infection or allergy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact reassessment and referral qualifiers support recording severe pain, immunocompromise, worsening, systemic illness, previous systemic reaction, sensitive-site, exotic-insect, fever and travel context only when assessed, plus clinician-discussed safety net and arranged review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting infection development, rapid worsening, systemic illness, severe disproportionate pain, previous systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, exotic exposure, fever, persistent lesions, and travel context.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact insect or arthropod, bite time and place, travel destination, lesion morphology and dimensions, allergy history, anaphylaxis criteria, tick and Lyme assessment, infection diagnosis, treatment, medicine, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
